page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 8 vCPUs. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, translating to a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It operates on a KVM hypervisor. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPU accelerators. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps, and no complimentary public IPv4 addresses are provided.

Benchmark results place this instance consistently in the average performance tier across most standard operations. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng yield scores of 1886.19 and 7715.37, respectively. Memory bandwidth is measured at 424.06 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 53.2 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 37.49 GB/sec for large-block writes. In application-specific tests, the server achieves a static web serving throughput of 9.04 GB/sec and handles 7.81 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.

From a resource-density perspective, the absence of bundled local storage and GPUs allows hardware allocation to focus entirely on memory capacity, offering a balanced profile for memory-bound tasks. While the instance performs adequately for smaller language models, processing 720.03 tokens per second for a 135M parameter model, it exhibits poor performance on medium-sized 7B models, with text generation dropping to 5.96 tokens per second. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 and solid database operation benchmarks, this server is appropriate for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, caching layers like Redis, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.2xlarge is an x86_64 virtual server running on a KVM hypervisor, featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores at 2.5 GHz and 64.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, making it appropriate for memory-intensive workloads. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks generally fall within the average tier, with a PassMark CPU score of 10405.69 and a Redis SET throughput of 7.81 million operations per second. However, it exhibits poor performance on medium-sized LLM inference tasks. This server is best utilized for database operations, caching layers, and memory-optimized enterprise applications that do not require local NVMe storage or specialized accelerators.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.2xlarge is an x86_64 server with 8 Intel Xeon cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, delivering average performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.2xlarge is an x86_64 KVM server with 8 Intel Xeon cores and 64 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio, optimized for database operations and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
64.0 GB of RAM providing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Average performance across database and Redis caching benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for memory-optimized workloads and database hosting
